title: thuja occidentalis f. sempervirens
intro: Thuja occidentalis f. sempervirens, also known as eastern white cedar, arborvitae, or American arborvitae, is a variety of the thuja occidentalis plant, specifically a foliage form that is known for its dark green, scale-like leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of thuja occidentalis f. sempervirens have been found to provide health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
